Title: Evolution and diversification of carboxylesterase-like [4+2] cyclases in aspidosperma and iboga alkaloid biosynthesis
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrHID3 directly cyclizes dehydrosecodine/angryline to tabersonine in vitro. 2. It represents the ancestral-like aspidosperma scaffold-forming cyclase activity among extant enzymes tested. 3. Co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana converted low endogenous/promiscuously generated angryline into tabersonine, supporting pathway reconstitution.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HID-like class I carboxylesterase (CXE); alpha/beta-hydrolase fold
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes regioselective [4+2]-type cyclization of dehydrosecodine (assayed as angryline) to form (-)-tabersonine.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves/whole plant specialized metabolism; not specifically resolved in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with angryline and LC-MS product identification; phylogenetic comparison; heterologous transient expression/pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful scaffold-forming enzyme for engineering aspidosperma alkaloid production such as tabersonine/vinblastine precursor pathways in heterologous hosts; likely stack with PAS/DPAS-like upstream enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Sequence mining from genomes/transcriptomes including OneKP; GenBank accessions PP133274-PP133316 for genes tested/cloned
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition with iboga-branch formation from shared dehydrosecodine substrate; no growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch-forming scaffold-generation step downstream of dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor to aspidosperma alkaloids including anticancer vinblastine/vincristine branch metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously referred to as TS and also used as phylogenetic search query; lacks canonical catalytic His and contains cyclase-specific active-site features
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays at 37 C in Tris pH 9.0; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves after stemmadenine acetate infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: PP133274-PP133316 range includes tested genes; specific locus NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrHID1 cyclizes dehydrosecodine/angryline to catharanthine. 2. It is the only CS activity identified among tested cyclases and is specific to Catharanthus roseus. 3. Ancestral/mutational analysis supports emergence of CS after duplication and neofunctionalization from a CorS-like ancestor.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HID-like class I carboxylesterase (CXE); alpha/beta-hydrolase fold
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes regio- and stereoselective [4+2]-type cyclization of dehydrosecodine (assayed as angryline) to produce (+)-catharanthine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with angryline and LC-MS; phylogenetics; ancestral sequence reconstruction and mutagenesis informed by crystal structure
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical for synthetic biology of catharanthine and downstream vinblastine precursor production in engineered systems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Sequence mining from genomes/transcriptomes including OneKP; GenBank accessions PP133274-PP133316 for genes tested/cloned
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shares dehydrosecodine precursor with tabersonine/coronaridine branches; no organismal tradeoff measured
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch-forming scaffold-generation step downstream of dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Catharanthine is a key precursor for anticancer bisindole alkaloids such as vinblastine/vincristine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structure cited as PDB 6RT8; evolved exclusively in Catharanthus genus from CorS-like ancestor
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays at 37 C in Tris pH 9.0
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: PP133274-PP133316 range includes tested genes; specific locus NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrHID2 forms 16-carbomethoxycleaviminium (16-cmc) from dehydrosecodine/angryline in vitro. 2. This identifies CorS-like activity in C. roseus in addition to TS and CS. 3. Evolutionary analysis indicates optimization from a low-activity ancestral CorS after duplication.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HID-like class I carboxylesterase (CXE); alpha/beta-hydrolase fold
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first C-C bond-forming cyclization of dehydrosecodine (assayed as angryline) to yield iminium intermediate 16-carbomethoxycleaviminium.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with angryline and LC-MS; phylogenetics; ancestral reconstruction and mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering iboga/pseudoaspidosperma alkaloid pathways when combined with appropriate downstream redox enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Sequence mining from genomes/transcriptomes including OneKP; GenBank accessions PP133274-PP133316 for genes tested/cloned
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux competition with TS and CS from same dehydrosecodine pool
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch-forming scaffold-generation step downstream of dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Coronaridine is an iboga alkaloid related to anti-addiction ibogaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CorS is mechanistically not a true full [4+2] cyclase because downstream oxidation/reduction completes scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays at 37 C in Tris pH 9.0
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: PP133274-PP133316 range includes tested genes; specific locus NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TiHID2 is confirmed as CorS, producing 16-cmc from angryline. 2. It underpins iboga and pseudoaspidosperma alkaloid biosynthesis in T. iboga. 3. Comparative phylogeny places this enzyme in an independently evolved CorS lineage relative to Catharanthus.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HID-like class I carboxylesterase (CXE); alpha/beta-hydrolase fold
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dehydrosecodine (assayed as angryline) to 16-carbomethoxycleaviminium, the immediate intermediate for coronaridine/pseudotabersonine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay and LC-MS product detection; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for iboga alkaloid pathway engineering and semisynthetic production of anti-addiction alkaloid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Sequence mining from genomes/transcriptomes including OneKP; GenBank accessions PP133274-PP133316 for genes tested/cloned
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux competition with tabersonine branch; no physiological penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch-forming scaffold-generation step downstream of dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides precursor to iboga alkaloids with anti-addiction relevance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized before this study; used as standard for 16-cmc product assignment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays at 37 C in Tris pH 9.0
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes 16-cmc formation leading to coronaridine/pseudotabersonine branch
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: PP133274-PP133316 range includes tested genes; specific locus NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TdHID2 exhibited high angryline cyclization activity to produce 16-cmc as the major product. 2. This supports CorS-like function in Tabernaemontaneae species beyond T. iboga. 3. The data support broader iboga/pseudoaspidosperma biosynthetic capacity in this lineage.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HID-like class I carboxylesterase (CXE); alpha/beta-hydrolase fold
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with angryline and LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative CorS-like catalyst for iboga alkaloid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GenBank accessions PP133274-PP133316 for genes tested/cloned
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch-forming scaffold-generation step downstream of dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core experimentally tested extant cyclase in Clade 1a
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays at 37 C in Tris pH 9.0
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: PP133274-PP133316 range includes tested genes; specific locus NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TeHID2 exhibited high angryline cyclization activity yielding 16-cmc. 2. It extends CorS-like activity to another Tabernaemontaneae species. 3. Supports independent diversification of iboga-branch cyclases in this lineage.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HID-like class I carboxylesterase (CXE); alpha/beta-hydrolase fold
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes dehydrosecodine (assayed as angryline) to the iminium intermediate 16-cmc.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with angryline and LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative CorS-like enzyme for heterologous iboga alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GenBank accessions PP133274-PP133316 for genes tested/cloned

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In Nicotiana benthamiana, CrDPAS enabled formation of angryline/dehydrosecodine from stemmadenine acetate when coupled with endogenous PAS-like activity. 2. Co-expression with TS converted this intermediate to tabersonine. 3. This supports a role for DPAS-like enzymes in supplying cyclase substrate during pathway evolution and engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces precondylocarpine acetate to generate dehydrosecodine/angryline precursor pool in MIA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Nicotiana benthamiana leaves in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with metabolite profiling by LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important upstream enzyme for reconstructing dehydrosecodine supply in engineered hosts for tabersonine/catharanthine/coronaridine production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GenBank accessions PP133274-PP133316 for genes tested/cloned
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports production of pharmaceutically relevant MIA scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in partial heterologous reconstitution; PAS activity supplied by endogenous N. benthamiana oxidase(s)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves after stemmadenine acetate infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases dehydrosecodine availability and enables tabersonine formation with TS
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: PP133274-PP133316 range includes tested genes; specific locus NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrTHAS1, although normally acting upstream in another MIA branch, generated small amounts of angryline in Nicotiana benthamiana. 2. Co-expression with TS converted this product to tabersonine. 3. The result supports enzyme promiscuity contributing to underground metabolism and pathway emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promiscuously reduces precondylocarpine acetate to generate low levels of dehydrosecodine/angryline.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Nicotiana benthamiana leaves in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates alternative ADH modules that may be repurposed for MIA pathway engineering, though likely less efficient than DPAS
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GenBank accessions PP133274-PP133316 for genes tested/cloned
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely low efficiency and off-pathway promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports production of downstream aspidosperma alkaloid scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence used to support underground metabolism model rather than dedicated natural pathway role
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves after stemmadenine acetate infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables low-level tabersonine production when combined with TS
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: PP133274-PP133316 range includes tested genes; specific locus NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrHYS generated small amounts of angryline in Nicotiana benthamiana despite being an upstream ADH from another MIA branch. 2. Co-expression with TS converted this intermediate to tabersonine. 3. This supports the proposed underground metabolism scenario for aspidosperma alkaloid emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promiscuously reduces precondylocarpine acetate to low levels of dehydrosecodine/angryline.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Nicotiana benthamiana leaves in transient assay

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrGS1 generated small amounts of angryline/dehydrosecodine in Nicotiana benthamiana. 2. Co-expression with TS yielded tabersonine. 3. The study cites GS as a dual-function enzyme and uses it to support pathway emergence through promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promiscuously/dually reduces precondylocarpine acetate to dehydrosecodine/angryline.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Nicotiana benthamiana leaves in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative enzyme module for engineering dehydrosecodine supply in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GenBank accessions PP133274-PP133316 for genes tested/cloned
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuous activity may reduce pathway specificity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports synthesis of pharmaceutically valuable MIA scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as dual-function enzyme in prior work and here leveraged for partial pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves after stemmadenine acetate infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables tabersonine formation when paired with TS
